vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2203: Lean swarm orchestrator: deterministic wave-driver over the swarm-v2 primitives (deferred PAN-1762 follow-up)

plan.title: Lean swarm orchestrator: deterministic wave-driver over the swarm-v2 primitives
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: PAN-1762 shipped the swarm-v2 primitives (dispatch-tier, swarm-readiness, resolveRegisteredSlotSpawn, verifyAndMergeSlot, reconcileSlotState) but ZERO production callers wire them into a loop — verifyAndMergeSlot / reconcileSlotState / spawnRun({slotIndex}) have only test callers, analyzeSwarmReadiness is called only by the finalize readiness report, and there is no `pan swarm` command. Parallel intra-issue execution therefore only works if a work-agent LLM hand-executes the prose foreman protocol at roles/work.md:70-107 — fragile and unsafe unattended. The full old orchestrator (src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts, 2598 lines) was deleted in 59f1e6cd4a partly for cause (no recovery when a slot's branch becomes unmergeable, PAN-1194).
narratives.Proposal: Turn the prose foreman loop into a deterministic, deacon-integrated coordination pass over the existing primitives: dispatch wave -> detect completion -> verify+merge -> advance -> recover. A new coordinateSwarmSlots() pass in src/lib/cloister/deacon-swarm.ts runs each runPatrol() tick (successor to the deleted detectMergedSwarmSlots patrol), with an optional agent.stopped reactive fast-path for near-instant advance. A thin `pan swarm <id>` CLI validates readiness and dispatches wave 0, then hands ongoing coordination to the deacon. Derive-don't-store: each tick re-derives merged/in-flight/pending from live git slot branches + tmux + the immutable main-side vBRIEF via reconcileSlotState; the only durable state is the vBRIEF item status the pipeline already owns. The deleted route is the LOGIC reference (auto-advance, cooldown, failed-merge recovery), not the revival — its stateful bloat is dropped.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Remote Fly slots (tracked in PAN-1773, Phase 2; depends on this + PAN-1676)
- Difficulty/model routing, tier table, and supervisor feeds (PAN-1791's axis; layers on top of this loop, sequenced after)
- Cross-issue concurrency slot manager (PAN-1666; different axis, interacts only at the shared local agent-pool ceiling)
- Reviving the deleted stateful SwarmState.slots array or its dual continue.json/sidecar persistence (explicitly rejected by D1/NFR-1)
- Any new HTTP route or dashboard-server endpoint for swarm coordination (deacon-integrated by D2)
- Direct mutation of the main-side vBRIEF spec (forbidden by the PAN-1124 single-spec-on-main invariant)
- Per-slot code review gate before merge — v1 slots merge on their verify_commands passing only; the issue's single downstream PR is reviewed once on the merged result, which is load-bearing (D7). Per-unit review is deliberately out of scope for v1.

autoDecisions.summary: Accepted the PRD's locked design decisions D1-D6 verbatim as the plan basis without operator confirmation (auto mode).
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD (.pan/drafts/pan-2203.md) is an explicit implementation brief whose file paths, symbols, and insertion points were re-verified accurate against main on 2026-06-30 during this session. No contradiction between the issue body and the PRD was found, so the auto-mode default is to execute the brief as written.

autoDecisions.summary: Split PRD work item WI-10 into three independent beads: wi-10-dashboard (per-slot dashboard identity, PAN-1263), wi-10-verdict (verdict->owning-slot routing, PAN-1472), and wi-10-gc (terminal slot GC, PAN-1202).
autoDecisions.rationale: The decompose-aggressively planning guidance says folded-issue bundles should be split into independently reviewable/mergeable beads; these three touch different subsystems (frontend tree vs verdict routing vs git worktree GC) and fold three distinct old issues, so each is separately testable.

autoDecisions.summary: Marked most implementation beads readiness:sequential rather than ready, and only wi-1 as readiness:ready.
autoDecisions.rationale: wi-2..wi-7, wi-9, and wi-10-gc all edit the same new file src/lib/cloister/deacon-swarm.ts; dispatching them as concurrent slots would collide on that file. Honest readiness classification serializes them behind the wi-1 foundation. This epic is largely a single-file build-up, not a wide parallel swarm.

autoDecisions.summary: Assigned requiresInspection:true to exactly two beads — wi-1 (deep) and wi-4 (fast).
autoDecisions.rationale: wi-1 defines the coordinateSwarmSlots module + signature + enumerate contract that every other bead builds on (foundation); wi-4 defines the capacity+overlap+claim-before-spawn dispatch contract that wi-5 (recovery re-dispatch) and wi-7 (synthesis dispatch) encode assumptions about. All other beads are leaf/mechanical or fail-fast at typecheck/review, so they default to false.

items.title: deacon-swarm.ts skeleton + coordinateSwarmSlots enumerate-swarms + patrol wiring

metadata.parallelSafeReason: wi-1 is the sole wave-0 foundation bead; it runs alone (nothing else dispatches concurrently), so its edits to the new deacon-swarm.ts cannot collide with any sibling.

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/cloister/deacon-swarm.ts exporting `coordinateSwarmSlots(opts?: {issueId?: string}): Promise<string[]>`. Implement enumerate-swarms (PRD 3.2 step 1): listFeatureWorkspaces() (deacon.ts:878) -> {issueId, workspacePath}; skip -slot-N sub-workspaces; keep issues whose main-side vBRIEF has analyzeSwarmReadiness(doc).swarmEligible === true (replaces the deleted discoverActiveSwarmIssueIds). Return [] when no swarms. Wire the 3-line call site (PRD 3.1 After) into runPatrol() (deacon.ts:2571) between checkFailedMergeRetry (deacon.ts:2899) and reconcileStaleMergeStatus (deacon.ts:2904), following the deacon.ts:2894-2901 idiom; import + re-export coordinateSwarmSlots alongside the other cloister passes (deacon.ts:35 import cluster / deacon.ts:142 re-export cluster), following the deacon-merge.ts module pattern. Async primitives only.

items.title: Given a feature workspace whose main-side vBRIEF has >=2 slot-eligible items, coordinateSwarmSlots() enumerates it and includes its issueId in the returned actions/considered set
Given a single-item or non-swarm-eligible vBRIEF, coordinateSwarmSlots() excludes that issue; given no feature workspaces, it returns an empty array
Given one patrol tick, runPatrol calls coordinateSwarmSlots exactly once between checkFailedMergeRetry and reconcileStaleMergeStatus, and the existing deacon patrol suite passes
Given the new module, deacon.ts re-exports coordinateSwarmSlots and typecheck passes without errors
Reconcile slot state + deterministic completion detection

narrative.Action: Per enumerated swarm, call reconcileSlotState(issueId, workspace, doc) (slot-reconcile.ts:52) to derive {merged, inFlight, pending} from live git slot branches (listSlotBranches, slot-reconcile.ts:100) + tmux slot agents (listSlotAgents, slot-reconcile.ts:114). For each in-flight slot, classify as `running` / `ready-to-merge` / `failed` by cross-checking listSessionNames (tmux.ts:970) + isPaneDead (tmux.ts:1237) + pane exit status against agent-<issue>-slot-N: pane exited 0 -> ready-to-merge; non-zero -> failed; vanished session -> failed. Inject the tmux/reconcile dependency so it is fakeable in tests. Async primitives only.

items.title: Given a slot whose pane exited 0, the classifier returns `ready-to-merge` for that slot
Given a slot whose pane exited non-zero, the classifier returns `failed`
Given a slot whose tmux session has vanished, the classifier returns `failed`; a slot with a live running pane returns `running`
Verify + no-ff merge ready slots, then mark the vBRIEF item done via the write door

narrative.Action: For each ready-to-merge slot (pane exit 0, branch unmerged), call verifyAndMergeSlot(issueId, slotIndex, item) (slot-merge.ts:36) — it runs item.metadata.verify_commands in the slot workspace then git merge --no-ff feature/<issue>-slot-N into the feature workspace. On merged:true, apply the canonical vBRIEF `done` for the item via applyTaskOperationToPlanFile (dag-cli.ts:140, the write door — the same op the deleted onSlotMergeComplete made). Emit `[swarm] merged slot N (item X) for <issue>`. On conflicts:true, emit a failed-merge action and leave the item claimed for recovery (deferred to wi-5). Never mutate the main-side spec directly.

items.title: Given verifyAndMergeSlot returns merged:true, the slot's vBRIEF item status flips to `done` through applyTaskOperationToPlanFile (write door) and a `[swarm] merged slot` action is emitted
Given verifyAndMergeSlot returns merged:false, no vBRIEF item status write occurs
Given verifyAndMergeSlot returns conflicts:true, the item is left claimed (not marked done) and a failed-merge action is emitted
Next-wave dispatch: capacity + files_scope overlap selection + claim-before-spawn

narrative.Action: Compute mergedItemIds from reconcile `merged` bucket -> getDispatchableItems(doc, mergedItemIds) (dag.ts:217). For each newly-ready item with a free slot index and no live slot agent: run capacity+overlap selection — defer by assertRegisteredSlotCap ceiling (spawn.ts:766) or by files_scope overlap against running+selected items (from analyzeSwarmReadiness().overlapMatrix). Allocate the lowest free slot index; reserve budget with tryReserveAdvancingSlot() (concurrency.ts, imported deacon.ts:147); claim the item via applyTaskOperationToPlanFile type `claim`; then spawnRun(issueId, 'work', {slotIndex, slotItemId}) (spawn.ts:78). On spawn throw, release the claim (unblock) AND release the advancing-slot budget — no orphan claims. Slot worktrees are created only via ensureRegisteredSlotWorktree (inside spawnRun); never git checkout inside a workspace.

items.title: Given two non-overlapping newly-dispatchable items and free capacity, exactly two slots are dispatched (2 spawnRun calls with distinct slotIndex)
Given two items whose files_scope overlap, exactly one is dispatched and the other is deferred (not dispatched concurrently)
Given the registered-slot cap is reached, additional dispatchable items are deferred rather than spawned
Given spawnRun throws, the item's claim is released (unblock) and the reserved advancing-slot budget is released
Unmergeability detection + failed-slot recovery (retry / drop / handoff)

narrative.Action: On verifyAndMergeSlot conflicts:true, transition the slot to `failed-merge` and block auto-advance for that issue until resolved (D6 / PAN-1194 — never silently halt or skip). Implement the three recovery actions the deleted recoverSwarmCommand had: retry (unblock the item + re-dispatch via the wi-4 dispatch path), drop (mark the item `done` via the write door + let the wave proceed), handoff (pause auto-advance + record an operator note). Expose these as a function the wi-8 `pan swarm recover` CLI subcommand calls.

items.title: Given a slot with conflicts:true, the slot becomes `failed-merge` and the issue's auto-advance is blocked (no further wave dispatch) until recovery runs
Given a failed-merge slot and action=retry, the item is unblocked and re-dispatched through the wi-4 dispatch path
Given a failed-merge slot and action=drop, the item is marked done and the wave proceeds; given action=handoff, auto-advance stays paused with an operator note recorded
Auto-advance cadence: idempotency + per-slot cooldown + per-issue backoff

narrative.Action: Add loop-safety to coordinateSwarmSlots: a module-level per-slot-branch refire cooldown map (like the deleted recentSlotMergeFires / SLOT_MERGE_REFIRE_COOLDOWN_MS) so an in-flight merge is not re-fired within the tick window; a per-issue failure counter that, after >=3 consecutive advance failures, imposes a 60s cooldown before retrying (like recordAutoAdvanceFailure / isAutoAdvanceCoolingDown); and a guard against re-dispatching a live (slot,item). All delay/cooldown tests MUST use vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync() — never real setTimeout (NFR-4).

items.title: Given a merge fired for a slot-branch this tick, a subsequent tick within the cooldown window does not re-fire the merge for that branch
Given 3 consecutive advance failures for an issue, the next advance is suppressed until 60s elapse, verified via vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync (fake timers)
Given a (slot,item) already live, coordinateSwarmSlots does not issue a second spawnRun for the same pair
Synthesis phase for DAG convergence items

narrative.Action: When a convergence item (>1 blocking parent — blockingParentCount (dag.ts:255) > 1, flagged requiresSynthesis by deriveSynthesisMetadata (dag.ts:270)) first becomes dispatchable with no persisted synthesis output, spawn a synthesis-phase slot that summarizes the parents' outputs into a context blob. On its completion, capture that context and dispatch the implementation slot carrying it via createActiveSlice(..., {synthesisOutputs}) (dag.ts:452). The implementation slot must not dispatch before the synthesis output exists.

items.title: Given a dispatchable item with >1 blocking parent and no synthesis output, a synthesis-phase slot is dispatched first (before the implementation slot)
Given the synthesis slot has completed, the implementation slot is dispatched with the synthesis context present in its active slice
Given a single-parent item, no synthesis slot is dispatched and the item goes straight to implementation
pan swarm CLI command (trigger + recover) + /pan-swarm skill

narrative.Action: Create src/cli/commands/swarm.ts and register it in src/cli/index.ts. `pan swarm <id>`: resolve id -> load main-side plan -> analyzeSwarmReadiness; if not swarmEligible, print why and exit non-zero; else ensure the feature workspace exists (reuse pan start's workspace setup) and dispatch wave 0 by calling the SAME first-wave selection the deacon uses (D3), then print the dispatched slots and hand ongoing coordination to the deacon. `pan swarm recover <id> <slotIndex> --action retry|drop|handoff` calls the wi-5 recovery function. Add skills/pan-swarm/SKILL.md (wrapper skill) and keep its flags/subcommands in sync so scripts/lint-skills.sh passes.

items.title: Given a non-swarm-eligible plan, `pan swarm <id>` prints the reason and exits non-zero without dispatching
Given a swarm-eligible plan, `pan swarm <id>` ensures the workspace and dispatches wave 0 (invokes the shared first-wave selection)
Given `pan swarm recover <id> <slot> --action retry`, the command spawns the recovery path for that slot
scripts/lint-skills.sh exits 0 with skills/pan-swarm/SKILL.md present and its documented flags matching `pan swarm --help`
Reactive agent.stopped fast-path for near-instant swarm advance

narrative.Action: In the agent.stopped handler in src/lib/cloister/service.ts (handleCloisterDomainEventPromise, ~line 423), when the stopped agentId matches ^agent-<issue>-slot-N$, call coordinateSwarmSlots({issueId}) so the swarm advances without waiting for the next 60s patrol. Non-slot agents must not trigger it. This is additive; the patrol remains the safety net (D4).

items.title: Given an agent.stopped event whose agentId matches agent-<issue>-slot-N, exactly one coordinateSwarmSlots({issueId}) call is made for that issue
Given an agent.stopped event for a non-slot agent, coordinateSwarmSlots is not called
Per-slot dashboard identity (surface agent-<issue>-slot-N on the issue tree/drawer)

narrative.Action: Give each slot a distinct, navigable dashboard identity (folds PAN-1263). Ensure resource-discovery (src/dashboard/server/services/resource-discovery.ts) nests agent-<issue>-slot-N agents under their issue, and that the frontend tree/drawer (SessionNode.tsx, DrawerAgentSession.tsx) render slot agents as distinct rows with openable terminals on the issue's pipeline row / drawer. Verify exact frontend anchor files during work (files_scope_confidence: medium).

items.title: Given a swarm with slots agent-<issue>-slot-1 and slot-2 running, resource-discovery nests both under the issue and the frontend renders two distinct slot rows
Given a rendered slot row, it exposes the slot's terminal/session as an openable navigable identity (not collapsed into the parent)
Route inspection/review verdicts to the owning slot agent

narrative.Action: Route inspection/review verdicts to the owning slot agent (agent-<issue>-slot-N), not a missing parent agent (folds PAN-1472). Where verdict feedback is delivered (review-verdict-feedback.ts / test-verdict.ts), resolve the target from the item's owning slot (slotItemId -> slotIndex) and deliver to agent-<issue>-slot-N via the async delivery primitive (sendKeysAsync / deliverAgentMessage). Confirm exact routing call sites during work (files_scope_confidence: medium).

items.title: Given a verdict for an item owned by slot N, the feedback is delivered to agent-<issue>-slot-N
Given the parent (non-slot) agent no longer exists, the verdict still reaches the owning slot rather than being dropped or targeted at the missing parent
Terminal (merged) slot worktree + branch GC after wave convergence

narrative.Action: After a wave's items are all `merged`, prune the merged slots' worktrees + branches (folds PAN-1202). In coordinateSwarmSlots, once reconcile shows a slot's item merged and its branch integrated, remove the slot worktree (git worktree remove) and delete the slot branch, using async primitives. Only merged/terminal slots are GC'd — running or failed-merge slots are never pruned.

items.title: Given a slot whose item is merged and branch integrated, its worktree and slot branch are removed
Given a running or failed-merge slot, its worktree and branch are preserved (not GC'd)
Rewrite docs/SWARM.md for swarm-v2 + the coordinateSwarmSlots orchestrator

expected_outputs: docs/SWARM.md describes coordinateSwarmSlots, pan swarm, recovery, and derive-don't-store

narrative.Action: Rewrite docs/SWARM.md (folds PAN-1555) to describe the swarm-v2 model + this orchestrator: the coordinateSwarmSlots deacon loop (enumerate -> reconcile/detect -> verify+merge -> advance -> recover), the pan swarm CLI trigger and pan swarm recover, derive-don't-store (NFR-1), and note that PAN-1791 layers difficulty-tiers/model-routing on top. Confirm the CLAUDE.md work-agents-via-pan `pan swarm <id>` reference is now accurate against the shipped command.

items.title: docs/SWARM.md contains sections covering the coordinateSwarmSlots loop stages, the pan swarm and pan swarm recover commands, and the derive-don't-store design
The doc states remote Fly slots (PAN-1773) and difficulty/model routing (PAN-1791) are out of scope / layered on top, and scripts/lint-skills.sh exits 0
Stalled-slot detection + duplicate-spawn guard

narrative.Action: Completion detection must NOT rely on pane-exit alone — a wedged-but-alive slot must never hang the wave (the kimi pane-alive-but-not-progressing problem). Track per-slot last-progress (branch-tip commit time and/or pane output). When a `running` slot exceeds a configurable stall threshold with no new commit on its branch AND no pane output, classify it `stalled` and escalate to recovery (retry/handoff, reusing the wi-5 recovery path). Duplicate-spawn guard (FR-18): before any (re)spawn for an item, assert no live agent-<issue>-slot-N session AND no unmerged feature/<issue>-slot-N worktree already exist for it — never double-spawn onto a reconnecting/paused slot (two agents on one worktree corrupt the tool_use/tool_result chain). All threshold/stall tests MUST use vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync() — never real setTimeout (NFR-4).

items.title: Given a running slot with no new branch commit and no pane output past the stall threshold, it is marked `stalled` and escalated to recovery (verified via vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync)
Given a fresh slot within the threshold, it stays `running` and is not marked stalled
Given a live agent-<issue>-slot-N session or an unmerged feature/<issue>-slot-N worktree already exists for an item, a (re)spawn for that item is refused
